description: Frozen evaluation substrate for all research-backlog experiments. Mirrors DEFAULT_STRICT_SEARCH_ORIGINS in recession_engine/backtester.py:90 as of git SHA ed24542. Every experiment must evaluate on this exact set to keep keep/discard verdicts comparable across iterations.

evaluation_scope_policy.policy: 1990-present is the PRIMARY evaluation window. Pre-1990 origins (Oil Crisis, Volcker I, Volcker II, 1978/1986 expansion checks) are retained as INFORMATIONAL — they report in all validation JSONs but do NOT drive KEEP/DISCARD verdicts. Training still uses full history (evaluation-only scope, not training-scope).
evaluation_scope_policy.rationale: Recurring pattern across A1/A1.75/B2/B2.5: modern-era optimization destabilizes pre-1990 detection. Pre-1990 macro regime (Volcker disinflation, 40% goods-producing employment share, pre-financial-innovation) is materially different from the regime the dashboard actually forecasts in. Optimizing for 1981 is museum work.
evaluation_scope_policy.gate_thresholds_adjustment: Smaller in-scope sample (~45 positive months vs full ~85) widens small-sample variance. Future experiments may widen CV-PR-AUC improvement gate slightly (e.g., 1.5pp instead of 2pp) — document per-experiment.

baseline_artifacts_note: CURRENT baseline (post-AUDIT-1 fix sweep). Rebaselined 2026-04-24 after all 5 audit fixes applied: deterministic feature-selection tie-break, threshold-plateau diagnostics in run_manifest, JOLTS lag 2→3 months, Lead_Months_Fixed elevated as primary lead metric, and PUBLICATION_LAGS applied to training path (training now matches backtest information set). Fresh retrain reproduces the 'good state' numbers from baseline_efb307e (CV PR-AUC 0.288, threshold 0.41), confirming the audit's finding that the Apr 23→24 drift was a threshold tie-break artifact and not a training-path defect.

baseline_metrics_at_freeze.source: data/models/{metrics.csv, backtest_summary.txt, run_manifest.json} at git SHA 2bd43e3 produced by `python3 -m scheduler.update_job` on 2026-04-24T16:36:02Z

S&L Crisis.note: crosses own threshold 0.31 at 1989-08 (+5.95mo pre-recession); crosses fixed 0.41 only at 1990-08 (after recession start 1990-07), so fixed-threshold lead goes negative. S&L signal is real but 0.41 is too high a bar for it.

Dot-com.note: peak 0.225 < own threshold 0.28 < fixed 0.41; never crosses either. A1.75.2's structural finding holds: Dot-com raw peak sits in the noise floor.

GFC.note: crosses own threshold 0.11 at 2006-12 (+6.96mo pre-recession) — strong underlying signal from B3 SLOOS features; crosses fixed 0.41 only at 2007-08 (1mo post-recession), so fixed-threshold lead is -1.02mo. Illustrates the AUDIT-1 finding: at fixed 0.41 the model looks late, at own threshold it's early. Underlying signal is real either way.

baseline_metrics_at_freeze.threshold_method: calibrated training F1 with precision/recall tie-breaks

threshold_diagnostics.note: Plateau is still wide (10 thresholds within 0.01 F1) but below the flat-warning threshold (width_005 = 4 < 5). Any challenger should be evaluated at the fixed 0.41 reference to avoid plateau-flip artifacts; AUDIT-1 FIX 4 now makes Lead_Months_Fixed the primary metric in backtest_summary.txt.

baseline_metrics_at_freeze.fixed_threshold_source: Current baseline's own threshold AND the C5 gate reference AND baseline_efb307e's threshold — all converge at 0.41. Kept stable for all future challenger comparisons.
baseline_metrics_at_freeze.deployed_ensemble_calibrator_note: A1.5 safety gate still passes. A1.75.2 DISCARD still applies — ensemble calibration is fragile; Dot-com unlock requires raising the raw peak, not better calibration.
baseline_metrics_at_freeze.training_info_set_note: FIX 5 (AUDIT-1) now applies PUBLICATION_LAGS to the training path. Training and backtest and live scoring all use the same information set. CV metrics are now realistic; no more perfect-foresight training optimism.
baseline_metrics_at_freeze.metrics_scope_note: Under the 1990-present scope policy, in-scope gate-relevant recessions are {S&L, Dot-com, GFC, COVID}. Post-fix state: S&L own-threshold lead +5.95mo (signal real but doesn't clear 0.41); Dot-com still missed at any threshold (structural); GFC own-threshold lead +6.96mo at threshold 0.11, fixed-threshold lead -1.02mo at 0.41; COVID late. The gap between own-threshold and fixed-threshold leads reflects each recession's peak probability relative to the stability reference — S&L peaks at 0.727 (above 0.41) but only late, GFC peaks at 0.671 (above 0.41) but only at recession onset, etc.

baseline_metrics_at_freeze_legacy_0411da.description: Retired baseline at git SHA 0411da9, frozen 2026-04-24. Kept for history. This was the mid-drift state that motivated AUDIT-1.
baseline_metrics_at_freeze_legacy_0411da.note: Threshold dropped to 0.30 end of the wide F1 plateau; audit later showed this was a tie-break artifact, not a real shift.

baseline_metrics_at_freeze_legacy_efb307.description: Retired baseline at git SHA efb307e, frozen 2026-04-23. Kept for history as the post-B3 production reference + as the source of the C5 fixed-threshold gate value (0.41).

baseline_metrics_at_freeze_legacy_ed2454.description: Retired baseline metrics computed at git SHA ed24542 on 2026-04-22, pre-A1/B1/B2. Kept for historical reference only. Use `baseline_metrics_at_freeze` (the 8df6b9d block above) for all new comparisons.
baseline_metrics_at_freeze_legacy_ed2454.source: data/models/metrics.csv + backtest_summary.txt + alfred_vintage_summary.txt + confidence_intervals.json at git SHA ed24542

notes: Plan originally said 13 origins; actual count in backtester is 15 (7 positive + 8 expansion).
ECE + calibration slope/intercept are unmeasured at freeze; they become new metrics after A1 ships.
Oil Crisis (1972-11 training cutoff) currently fails with single-class error; keep in set as negative diagnostic.
2026-04-23: evaluation_scope policy added. Pre-1990 origins become informational; post-1990 drives gate decisions. Training window unchanged (full history).

2026-04-23 (A2 finding): simulated publication lags diverge from real ALFRED vintage by 6.65pp mean, 19.96pp max. Dot-com 2000-10 origin flips detection (simulated 46.4% crosses threshold; vintage 30.0% does not). All prior Dot-com analyses were measured under simulated lag — the real vintage picture is stricter. GFC/S&L/COVID are consistent across both pipelines. ALFRED vintage cache at `data/alfred_cache/` (189 parquets). Validation at `data/models/a2_validation.json`; summary at `data/models/a2_vintage_replay_summary.md`.
